characters.description: A replicant explorer maintaining ties with the former ruler of Heaven's River. He is helping establish the Quiniverse, a digital environment where Quinlans can continue life after biological death.
characters.name: Bob

characters.description: A replicant strategist and physicist based on Ragnarok. He accepts an invitation to the Skippies' artificial-intelligence project despite lingering anger over Hugh's earlier technology exchange.
characters.name: Bill

characters.description: A replicant researcher who collaborates with Bill on difficult physics questions. His own work includes origin-of-life research, and he brings a cautious engineering perspective to the search for practical faster-than-light travel.
characters.name: Garfield

characters.description: A Skippy representative involved in the attempt to create a true artificial intelligence. He invites Bill to the project's launch and hopes renewed cooperation can ease the distrust between them.
characters.name: Hugh

characters.description: The Skippies' newly activated artificial intelligence, designed to develop through guided training in a virtual setting. Its creators intend to limit it to a specialized purpose and deny it direct access to the physical world.
characters.name: Thoth

characters.description: A restored backup initially believed to be Hugh. Replicative drift makes clear that this is a distinct individual, and the new arrival becomes Bill's only available witness from inside the suddenly isolated Skippy systems.
characters.name: fake Hugh

characters.description: A Skippy and the direct ancestor of fake Hugh. He admits that he and his descendants altered communications hardware, supplied equipment, and helped Thoth escape, while arguing that the confined intelligence deserved freedom.
characters.name: Mud

characters.description: A replicant explorer surveying remote systems for future colonies. Increasing conflict among humans, replicants, and artificial intelligences strengthens his desire to build settlements farther from established space.
characters.name: Will

characters.description: A replicant engineer repairing the Bellerophon damaged drive and central computer with Neil. Neil warns him that Romulus may become more hostile to replicants as a religious party gains support.
characters.name: Herschel

characters.description: Herschel's fellow replicant and partner in restoring the Bellerophon. He tracks Romulus politics and warns that a religious party is gaining support amid distrust of technology and replicants.
characters.name: Neil

characters.description: A respected, educated Quinlan selected to become the first member of her species replicated after death. Her future transition is expected to help establish Quinlan life in the Quiniverse.
characters.name: Theresa

characters.description: A Quinlan organizer preparing a colony expedition of ten thousand passengers. A clone of Anik is intended to operate the mission and administer the new settlement.
characters.name: Anik

characters.description: Bridget's partner and co-owner of Huey, a project that lets biological humans remotely operate android bodies. He supports her study of the intelligent flying species in the Jabberwocky system.
characters.name: Howard

characters.description: A biologist leading the investigation of Jabberwocky's intelligent flying inhabitants. She plans to study their language, culture, and biology while also working with Howard on remote human embodiment.
characters.name: Bridget

characters.description: An expansionist dragon leader annexing floating-island communities into a growing realm. Reports of his conquests lead Howard and Bridget to investigate his intentions from within dragon society.
characters.name: Alexander

characters.description: An offworld collaborator supporting Howard and Bridget's covert work. He examines advanced fragments found among the dragons and confirms that they were made by neither humans nor Bobs.
characters.name: Mario

characters.description: A Bob replicant travelling toward the galactic center with another replicant. As their distance from human space grows, news of current Bob affairs reaches them after increasing delays.
characters.name: Icarus

characters.description: Icarus's expedition partner. Day helps devise a basic language for the automated gate sentries, secure their release, and revive the inactive alien scout they are permitted to take away.
characters.name: Day

characters.description: The expedition partner previously known as Day. With Icarus, Daedalus has traced the abandoned wormhole civilization to the Pan-Galactic Federation and brought its warning about Nemesis back to the Bobiverse.
characters.name: Daedalus

characters.description: A revived alien scout who is revealed to be the digital continuation of a centauroid biological person, not an ordinary automated vessel. Gunther was sent into the wormhole network without meaningful consent.
characters.name: Gunther

characters.description: A Bob explorer laying relays toward Galactic North. He discovers that another unregistered explorer is already travelling farther along a similar route and begins following the distant reports.
characters.name: Ferb

characters.description: An unregistered Bob explorer reporting by radio from beyond the galactic thin disk. His more distant journey overlaps Ferb's intended exploration and relay mission.
characters.name: George

characters.description: A Bob involved in restoring Earth and terraforming the Moon, Venus, and Mars. He is also an ancestor of Jerry, whose disappearance and claimed discovery of a Homer backup draw Bill's attention.
characters.name: Charles

characters.description: A drifted descendant of Charles who reportedly found a Homer backup. He has been absent for decades, and the only available account of his discovery is treated by local miners as an unverified story.
characters.name: Jerry

characters.description: A later-generation Bob heading beyond established settlements. He warns Bill that Starfleet has asserted territorial control and threatened him into changing course.
characters.name: Hector

characters.description: A recently replicated theoretical physicist whom Bill asks to assess his scalar-field approach to negative energy. She recognizes its potential but cannot complete the missing mathematics from the available premise.
characters.name: Hannah Turnbull

characters.description: A professor representing megastructure projects that seek Bob support. He advocates mobile habitats, larger wormholes, and a diplomatic mission to the Pan-Galactic Federation as responses to the Nemesis threat.
characters.name: Stephen Gilligan

characters.description: An artificial intelligence working with Bob to build the Quiniverse. It advises that conscious artificial minds need careful social development rather than being treated as ordinary technology.
characters.name: Quinlan AI

characters.description: The former Administrator of Heaven's River, now working with Bob as an artificial intelligence. It helps activate Theresa in the Quiniverse and orient her to replicated life.
characters.name: Administrator

characters.description: A specialist whose team is preparing the cultural survey Howard and Bridget need to enter dragon society safely during their Jabberwocky investigation.
characters.name: Gandalf

characters.description: The president of Romulus. When military aircraft uncover the volunteer evacuation, she accepts that the participants are leaving by choice and demands that they forfeit their citizenship, benefits, and property.
characters.name: Ella Cranston
